content: We can redesign retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) for autonomous AI agents by reverse-engineering the prefrontal-hippocampal memory interaction mechanism that underpins human memory retrieval, where the prefrontal cortex (agent's active goal-directed reasoning system) guides the hippocampus (RAG's external knowledge store) to retrieve only context-relevant memory fragments instead of exhaustive similarity search. This creates a curious active learning loop where the agent continuously updates its RAG knowledge index with high-value, information-gap filling data aligned to its ongoing task goals.
